title: St. Olav's Church, Serampore
tagline: St. Olav's Church, Serampore — spiritual town of West Bengal

heroImage.alt: St. Olav's Church, Serampore, West Bengal

overview.short: St. Olav's Church, Serampore is a much-loved spiritual town in West Bengal, known for temples and pilgrimage.
overview.description: St. Olav's Church (Bengali: সেন্ট ওলাভ'স চার্চ, romanized: Sēnṭa ōlābha'sa cārca) is a protestant church in Serampore, West Bengal, India. Also known as St Olave's Danish Church, it was built in the early 19th century, when the area was part of Fredriksnagore – then a small (50 km²) colony of Denmark-Norway. The building is now jointly controlled by the Church of North India (CNI) and Serampore College Council.

topPlaces.name: Serampore Municipality
topPlaces.description: Serampore Municipality is the civic body that governs Serampore and its surrounding areas (Mahesh, Tin Bazar, Battala and partly Chatra) in Srirampore subdivision of Hooghly district, West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Capture of Serampore (1808)
topPlaces.description: The Capture of Serampore (Danish: Overtagelsen af Frederiksnagore), also commonly known as the Second Capture of Serampure (Den anden overtagelse af Frederiksnagore), was a siege and capture of the Danish colony of Serampore by British forces on 28 January 1808. The Danes capitulated immediately after the arrival of the British, as the latter's forces were far superior.

topPlaces.name: British occupation of Serampore (1763)
topPlaces.description: The British occupation of Serampore also referred to as the English occupation of Serampore (Danish: Den engelske besættelse af Serampore) was a siege and thereafter a minor military occupation by the United Kingdom on the Danish trading post of Serampore (Frederiksnagore) in 1763.

topPlaces.name: Serampore
topPlaces.description: Serampore (also known as Serampur, Srirampur, Srirampore, Shreerampur, Shreerampore, Shrirampur or Shrirampore) is a city in Hooghly district of the Indian state of West Bengal. It serves as the headquarters of the Srirampore subdivision and is part of the area covered by Kolkata Metropolitan Development Authority (KMDA).

topPlaces.name: Capture of Serampore (1801)
topPlaces.description: The Capture of Serampore (Danish: Overtagelsen af Serampore), alternatively the Capture of Frederiknagore (Danish: Overtagelsen af Frederiknagore), was a British capture of the Danish colony of Serampore (Frederiknagore) on 8 May 1801 during the English Wars. The British met no resistance, and could subsequently quickly capture the settlement.

topPlaces.name: Battala
topPlaces.description: Battala is a highly populated area and a semi industrial neighbourhood in central Serampore of Hooghly district in the Indian state of West Bengal. It is a part of the area covered by Kolkata Metropolitan Development Authority (KMDA).

topPlaces.name: Chatra, Serampore
topPlaces.description: Chatra is a neighbourhood in Serampore of Hooghly district in the Indian state of West Bengal. It is a part of the area covered by Kolkata Metropolitan Development Authority (KMDA).

topPlaces.name: Kendriya Vidyalaya Barrackpore (Army)
topPlaces.description: PM Shri Kendriya Vidyalaya Barrackpore (KVB), is a school in Kolkata, West Bengal. The school is affiliated to the Central Board of Secondary Education, New Delhi.

items.note: Serampore Municipality is the civic body that governs Serampore and its surrounding areas (Mahesh, Tin Bazar, Battala and partly Chatra) in Srirampore subdivision of Hooghly district, West Bengal, India.

The Capture of Serampore (Danish: Overtagelsen af Frederiksnagore), also commonly known as the Second Capture of Serampure (Den anden overtagelse af Frederiksnagore), was a siege and capture of the Danish colony of Serampore by British forces on 28 January 1808. The Danes capitulated immediately after the arrival of the British, as the latter's forces were far superior.

The British occupation of Serampore also referred to as the English occupation of Serampore (Danish: Den engelske besættelse af Serampore) was a siege and thereafter a minor military occupation by the United Kingdom on the Danish trading post of Serampore (Frederiksnagore) in 1763.

Serampore (also known as Serampur, Srirampur, Srirampore, Shreerampur, Shreerampore, Shrirampur or Shrirampore) is a city in Hooghly district of the Indian state of West Bengal. It serves as the headquarters of the Srirampore subdivision and is part of the area covered by Kolkata Metropolitan Development Authority (KMDA).

The Capture of Serampore (Danish: Overtagelsen af Serampore), alternatively the Capture of Frederiknagore (Danish: Overtagelsen af Frederiknagore), was a British capture of the Danish colony of Serampore (Frederiknagore) on 8 May 1801 during the English Wars. The British met no resistance, and could subsequently quickly capture the settlement.

Battala is a highly populated area and a semi industrial neighbourhood in central Serampore of Hooghly district in the Indian state of West Bengal. It is a part of the area covered by Kolkata Metropolitan Development Authority (KMDA).
Chatra is a neighbourhood in Serampore of Hooghly district in the Indian state of West Bengal. It is a part of the area covered by Kolkata Metropolitan Development Authority (KMDA).
PM Shri Kendriya Vidyalaya Barrackpore (KVB), is a school in Kolkata, West Bengal. The school is affiliated to the Central Board of Secondary Education, New Delhi.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit St. Olav's Church, Serampore?
faq.a: The best time to visit St. Olav's Church, Serampore is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach St. Olav's Church, Serampore?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International Airport, Kolkata (16 km) and the nearest railway station is Howrah Junction (Kolkata) (19 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for St. Olav's Church, Serampore?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of St. Olav's Church, Serampore at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in St. Olav's Church, Serampore?
faq.a: Stays in St. Olav's Church, Serampore start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in St. Olav's Church, Serampore?
faq.a: The top attractions are Serampore Municipality, Capture of Serampore (1808), British occupation of Serampore (1763), Serampore, Capture of Serampore (1801).
